name: Current Affairs in Geography of India (5): The Subansiri Lower Hydroelectric Project

sourceRefs.label: drishtiias.com/waterpowermagazine.com - Subansiri Lower Hydroelectric Project progress (Unit 5 completed 14 June 2026; full commissioning expected Dec 2026)

researchNotes: Fifth Geography current-affairs topic (5th lap). India's largest hydropower project under construction — solid river-systems/North-East-India geography content with a concrete, dated progress milestone.

reading.intro: Covers India's largest under-construction hydropower project — the Subansiri Lower Hydroelectric Project — its location, capacity, and 2026 progress.

qa.question: Where is the Subansiri Lower Hydroelectric Project located, and on which river?
qa.answer: On the Subansiri River, at the border of Arunachal Pradesh and Assam — making it a North-East India hydropower project.

qa.question: What is the Subansiri Lower Project's total installed capacity, and how is it structured?
qa.answer: 2,000 MW total, comprising 8 units of 250 MW each. It is being implemented by NHPC Limited.

qa.question: What milestone did the project reach on 14 June 2026?
qa.answer: Successful 'boxing-up' of Unit 5 (250 MW), bringing the project's commissioned capacity to 1,250 MW across 5 units.

qa.question: When is the Subansiri Lower Project expected to be fully commissioned?
qa.answer: By December 2026, with the remaining units commissioned in a phased manner.

qa.question: How much electricity is the project expected to generate annually once complete?
